license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jqvcmw6
title: Sample embbeding with epoxy resin
author: Debora Ferreira Barreto-Vieira

steps_raw.instruction: Wash previously fixed cells in sodium cacodylate buffer 0,2M in saccharose 7% for 5 minutes (3 times).
Postfix cells in osmium tetroxide 1% for 30 minutes.
Wash cells in sodium cacodylate buffer 0,2M in saccharose 7% for 5 minutes (1 time) and in distilled water for 10 minutes (2 times).

Dehydrate cells by immersing the material in graduated solutions of acetone as follows: acetone 15%, 30% and 50% (10 minutes each), acetone 70% in uranyl acetate 1% (30 minutes), acetone 90% (5 minutes) and acetone 100% in copper sulfate (2 times for 10 minutes).
Infiltrate cells by immersing the material in epoxi resin at a 1:3 ratio of acetone P.A. for 30 minutes and at 3:1 ratio of acetone P.A overnight.
Embed cells by adding epoxi resin to the eppendorfs containing the cells. Polymerization occurs at 60 ° C during three days.
